notes: Added workflow guidance that markup-only class presence is not enough evidence for UI changes that introduce reusable semantic CSS classes.
Documented that verifier work should confirm the served stylesheet contains the expected selectors or rules when new semantic utilities are introduced.
Recorded the current repo-specific Tailwind v4 lesson: prefer plain CSS in globals.css for repo-local semantic utilities unless browser evidence proves the chosen layer pattern is emitted reliably.
open_issues: The workflow guidance captures the current repo behavior but does not yet automate CSS-rule verification in a dedicated action wrapper.
The preference for plain CSS is repo-specific and may need revisiting if the Tailwind or bundler setup changes.
